content:
First, factor \(391=17\cdot23\).  We want \(\mathrm{lcm}(a,b)>\mathrm{lcm}(a,391)\) with \(a<391\).  Writing 
\[
v_p(\mathrm{lcm}(x,y))=\max\{v_p(x),v_p(y)\},
\]
the inequality 
\[
\mathrm{lcm}(a,b)\;>\;\mathrm{lcm}(a,391)
\]
is equivalent to

1.  For every prime \(p\), 
   \(\displaystyle \max\{v_p(a),v_p(b)\}\ge\max\{v_p(a),v_p(391)\}\),
   so that no prime‐power in \(\mathrm{lcm}(a,391)\) is lost, and  
2.  For at least one prime \(p\), 
   \(\displaystyle \max\{v_p(a),v_p(b)\}>\max\{v_p(a),v_p(391)\}\).

Since \(391\) has exactly the primes \(17\) and \(23\), for each of these primes \(p\) with \(v_p(a)<1\) we must have \(v_p(b)\ge1\) (to avoid losing that factor).  But we cannot have \(a\) divisible by both \(17\) and \(23\) when \(a<391\), so \(b\) must supply whichever one of \(\{17,23\}\) is missing in \(a\).  To make the lcm strictly larger, \(b\) must also introduce a strictly larger exponent somewhere, and the cheapest way to do that is to use the prime \(2\).

Choose for instance
\[
a=23\quad(\text{so }v_{17}(a)=0,\;v_{23}(a)=1),
\]
and
\[
b=2\cdot17=34.
\]
Then
\[
\mathrm{lcm}(23,\,34)=2\cdot17\cdot23=782,
\quad
\mathrm{lcm}(23,\,391)=17\cdot23=391,
\]
and indeed \(782>391\).  One checks that no smaller \(b\) can work (below \(34\) you either fail to cover one of \(17,23\) or fail to get a strict increase). 

Hence the minimum possible \(b\) is 
\[
\boxed{34}.
\]

\]
is equivalent to

1.  For every prime \(p\), 
   \(\displaystyle \max\{v_p(a),v_p(b)\}\ge\max\{v_p(a),v_p(391)\}\),
   so that no prime‐power in \(\mathrm{lcm}(a,391)\) is lost, and  
2.  For at least one prime \(p\), 
   \(\displaystyle \max\{v_p(a),v_p(b)\}>\max\{v_p(a),v_p(391)\}\).

Since \(391\) has exactly the primes \(17\) and \(23\), for each of these primes \(p\) with \(v_p(a)<1\) we must have \(v_p(b)\ge1\) (to avoid losing that factor).
